mission.title: Install and Configure Vultr Kubernetes Engine (VKE)
mission.description: Vultr Kubernetes Engine (VKE) is a managed Kubernetes service that allows users to deploy and manage Kubernetes clusters on Vultr's infrastructure. It simplifies the setup process and integrates Vultr-specific features like load balancers and cloud controller management. Key trade-offs include reliance on Vultr's ecosystem and potential vendor lock-in.

steps.title: Install the Vultr CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Vultr command-line interface, which is necessary for managing your Vultr resources.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/vultr/vultr-cli/releases/download/v1.0.0/vultr-cli_linux_amd64.tar.gz

tar -xzf vultr-cli_linux_amd64.tar.gz

sudo mv vultr-cli /usr/local/bin/

vultr-cli version

```

> Download the latest Vultr CLI
> Extract the downloaded tarball
> Move the CLI to a directory in your PATH
> Verify the installation

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster on Vultr with the specified version and node count.

```bash

vultr-cli k8s create --region 'ewr' --version '1.30' --node-count 3 --plan 'vc2-2c-4gb'

```

> Replace 'ewr' with your desired region

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to interact with the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

vultr-cli k8s kubeconfig --cluster-id <your-cluster-id> > ~/.kube/config

```

> Replace <your-cluster-id> with the actual cluster ID

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in 'Ready' status

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step sets up additional features like autoscaling and monitoring for your cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes/autoscaler/master/cluster-autoscaler/cloudprovider/vultr/examples/cluster-autoscaler.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vultr/vultr-cloud-controller-manager/master/docs/examples/monitoring.yaml

```

> Install the cluster autoscaler
> Set up monitoring for your cluster

uninstall.title: Delete the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command will remove the Kubernetes cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this action cannot be undone and may leave orphaned resources.

```bash

vultr-cli k8s delete --cluster-id <your-cluster-id>

```

> Replace <your-cluster-id> with the actual cluster ID

uninstall.title: Remove the Vultr CLI
uninstall.description:
This command removes the Vultr CLI from your system.

```bash

sudo rm /usr/local/bin/vultr-cli

```

> Clean up the CLI installation

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your cluster data. This step will upgrade your cluster to the latest version.

```bash

vultr-cli k8s upgrade --cluster-id <your-cluster-id> --version '1.30'

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

```

> Replace <your-cluster-id> with the actual cluster ID
> Drain the node before upgrading
> Uncordon the node after upgrade

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: dial tcp <ip>:<port>: i/o timeout'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates a network issue or that the cluster is not running.

**Fix:**
Check your network settings and ensure the cluster is up and running with 'vultr-cli k8s list'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources available'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the selected plan does not have enough resources for the requested node count.

**Fix:**
Choose a higher plan or reduce the node count.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create load balancer'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can occur if the cloud controller manager is not properly configured.

**Fix:**
Ensure the Vultr Cloud Controller Manager is deployed and configured correctly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kubelet is not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the kubelet service on the node has failed.

**Fix:**
Check the node logs with 'kubectl logs <node-name>' and restart the kubelet service.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Vultr instance types and improved load balancer integration.
versionNotes.deprecations: Removed support for deprecated instance types.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.29, ensure your configurations are compatible with the new instance types.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get nodes' showing all nodes in 'Ready' status, confirming that your cluster is operational.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding.
